families.intact_state_summary: The surrounding layout, local draft text, linked review context, and publish-later lineage remain intact.
families.notes: Provider-linked drafts keep a truthful placeholder and a preserved rollback target instead of silently reissuing managed writes.

placeholder_plan.summary: The draft stays visible in place with its lineage and retained compare target while publish and direct writes remain blocked.

families.safest_route_rationale: Rolling back to the preserved draft is safer than reset or rebuild because this family carries user-authored content that the product does not own.

families.intact_state_summary: Workspace chrome, surrounding tabs, pinned inputs, and redaction-safe request history remain open.
families.notes: Request workspace state keeps surrounding context and routes into compare-first repair rather than collapsing the whole workspace.

placeholder_plan.summary: Request panes reopen with their slot, identity, and request history intact while the broken document family waits for review.

families.safest_route_rationale: Request-workspace corruption can often be repaired or compared in place; a broader workspace reset would discard intact neighboring state for no gain.

families.intact_state_summary: The notebook pane, neighboring layout, and retained notebook lineage stay visible even when the structured notebook body is quarantined.
families.notes: Notebook placeholder continuity keeps the pane honest about blocked runtime state instead of silently dropping it from restore.

placeholder_plan.summary: Notebook panes keep their slot, identity, and notebook context while execution and writes stay blocked behind repair or rollback.

families.safest_route_rationale: Notebook state is durable workspace truth; guided repair preserves metadata and attachments more safely than any destructive rebuild.

families.intact_state_summary: The preview slot, route identity, and last-known metadata remain visible while only the broken cache shard is replaced.
families.notes: Broken preview cache shards narrow only the preview lane and reopen the slot with a truthful stale placeholder.

placeholder_plan.summary: Preview surfaces reopen with a stale-artifact placeholder and retained evidence while rebuild reruns from authoritative source state.

families.safest_route_rationale: Preview cache shards are derived state. Automatic rebuild is safer than rollback because the authoritative source remains elsewhere and user state is untouched.

families.intact_state_summary: Notebook outputs, exports, traces, and other generated artifacts keep their lineage, metadata, and surrounding layout even when current bytes are stale.
families.notes: Generated artifact drift is surfaced as stale or retained output, not as a silent claim that current output is still live truth.

placeholder_plan.summary: Generated outputs reopen as retained or stale artifacts with their lineage visible until regeneration succeeds or a repair review takes over.

families.safest_route_rationale: Generated artifacts derive from notebook, query, preview, or trace sources. Regeneration is the narrowest safe action and avoids rewriting durable source state.

families.intact_state_summary: Queued sync intent, preserved unsaved work, and surrounding activity context stay reachable even when replay must stop for review.
families.notes: Journal-preserved work must reopen as honest drafts or compare views; no managed action replays invisibly.

placeholder_plan.summary: Journal-backed work reopens as recovered drafts with compare-first repair rather than replaying or discarding queued mutations.

families.safest_route_rationale: Recovery journals may hold unsent or unpublished user intent. Guided repair and recovered-draft placeholders are safer than reset because they preserve user-owned work for review.

families.intact_state_summary: Ordinary local editing, layout context, and last-known managed metadata remain intact even while privileged routes stay blocked.
families.notes: Trust and policy failures narrow managed capabilities in place instead of erasing surrounding context or silently widening authority.

placeholder_plan.summary: Managed surfaces stay visible with a blocked-privileged placeholder so local editing continues while trust or policy state is repaired.

families.safest_route_rationale: Corrupted trust or policy state must fail closed for privileged operations only. Blocking the entire app would overreach when local editing remains safe.

invariants: Each covered state class degrades independently; damage in one family does not justify a wider workspace or whole-app reset.
Placeholder continuity preserves layout slot and logical identity when a family is missing, stale, quarantined, or under review.
Durable user and workspace state never rebuild automatically; derived caches and generated artifacts prefer rebuild because authoritative truth lives elsewhere.
Recovery journals preserve drafts and compare targets rather than silently replaying or discarding user-owned intent.
Trust and policy corruption fails closed for privileged operations while keeping ordinary local editing and support export available.
title: State-class recovery routing and placeholder continuity
